description: Stripe is a payments platform. KeyForge needs a restricted key with read access so it can verify the credential and report usage without ever moving money on your behalf.

steps.title: Open the Stripe API keys page
steps.description: Sign in to your Stripe account and go to Developers, API keys.

steps.title: Click Create restricted key
steps.description: In the Restricted keys section, click Create restricted key. Pick a descriptive name like KeyForge so future-you knows what this key is for.

steps.title: Pick the permissions
steps.description: Read access is enough for KeyForge to verify the credential. Pick Read for everything; you can tighten this later if you want to scope it down to just the resources you use.

steps.title: Copy the key and paste it below
steps.description: Click Reveal token, copy the full key (it starts with rk_test_ or rk_live_), then paste it below. KeyForge encrypts it at rest the moment you click Save.
